description: Enhanced messaging system with typing indicators, image sending, read receipts, group chat creation, and voice/video calling using native WebRTC with Firebase Firestore signaling.
steps: 1. Rewrite src/pages/Messages.jsx: add group chat creation button/modal (select multiple users, set group name), improve chat list to show unread count, last message preview, online status dot, distinguish group vs 1:1 chats, add floating action button for new chat/group

2. Rewrite src/pages/ChatView.jsx: add typing indicator (write to Firestore typing subcollection on input, listen for other user typing), add image sending via Cloudinary upload button, add read receipts (mark messages as read when chat is open, show double-check icon for read messages), add call buttons (voice/video) in chat header, show group members list for group chats

3. Create src/pages/VideoCall.jsx: Full-screen video call UI using native RTCPeerConnection API. Firebase Firestore as signaling server (store offer/answer/ICE candidates in a 'calls' collection). Features: local/remote video streams, mute audio, toggle camera, end call, call timer, caller/callee UI states, ringing state

4. Create src/pages/VoiceCall.jsx: Voice-only call UI using native RTCPeerConnection with audio-only constraints. Show caller avatar, call timer, mute button, speaker toggle, end call button. Same Firestore signaling pattern as video call

5. Create src/services/webrtc.js: Shared WebRTC utilities - createPeerConnection (with STUN servers), createOffer, createAnswer, addIceCandidate, Firestore signaling helpers (createCallDocument, listenForAnswer, listenForICECandidates, sendICECandidate)

6. Create src/components/IncomingCall.jsx: Overlay component that listens for incoming calls (Firestore listener on calls where recipientId matches current user and status is 'ringing'), shows caller info with accept/reject buttons, navigate to VideoCall or VoiceCall on accept
7. Update src/App.jsx: add routes for /call/video/:callId and /call/voice/:callId, render IncomingCall component globally inside AuthProvider
acceptance_criteria: Users can send text and image messages in real-time
Typing indicator shows when other user is typing
Read receipts show double-check marks for read messages
Users can create group chats with multiple participants
Voice call initiates WebRTC connection via Firestore signaling
Video call shows local and remote video streams
Incoming call overlay appears when someone calls
Call can be accepted or rejected
verification: Verify all files have valid JSX syntax
Verify WebRTC code uses only native browser APIs (RTCPeerConnection, getUserMedia)
Verify Firestore signaling pattern is correct (offer/answer/candidates flow)
findings: All 8 steps completed. Created webrtc.js service with STUN servers (stun.l.google.com:19302, stun1.l.google.com:19302), RTCPeerConnection helpers, and Firestore signaling (offer/answer/ICE candidates in calls collection). Messages.jsx enhanced with group chat creation modal, unread badges, online status dots, FAB menu. ChatView.jsx enhanced with typing indicators (writes to chats/{chatId}/typing/{uid}), image sending via Cloudinary, read receipts (lastRead map in chat doc), and voice/video call initiation. VideoCall.jsx has full-screen PiP layout. VoiceCall.jsx has avatar with pulse animation. IncomingCall.jsx listens for calls where recipientId matches and status is ringing. Routes added for /call/video/:callId and /call/voice/:callId. Cannot run npm build in INTEGRATIONS_ONLY mode - verified via brace/paren balance check and node --check on .js files.
